symptoms.symptom_name: Intermittent low-volume bright-red rectal bleeding coating the stool over three weeks
symptoms.character: bright red, coating the stool, dripping into the bowl, low volume

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distal colonic/rectal polyps bleed intermittently with low-volume bright-red blood.
Recurrent rectal bleeding over weeks is an alarm feature mandating colonoscopy.
Bright-red outlet bleeding is classic for haemorrhoids but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: New change in bowel habit (mild constipation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Change in bowel habit with bleeding raises pre-test probability of a colorectal neoplasm.
Larger or obstructing polyps can alter bowel habit.
Chronic GI blood loss produces iron deficiency and fatigue.
Iron-deficiency anaemia from occult GI loss is itself an indication for colonoscopy.
Absence of weight loss lowers but does not exclude malignancy — early cancer and polyps are typically weight-neutral.

symptoms.symptom_name: No severe abdominal pain, nausea or vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No colicky pain, distension or vomiting — no current obstructive complication.

symptoms.symptom_name: Haemodynamically stable — no dizziness/postural symptoms, no large clots or high-volume bleeding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No syncope, large clots or high-volume loss — not a current haemorrhagic emergency; keeps baseline at urgent-referral rather than emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdominal examination — soft, non-tender, no masses or hepatomegaly (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician finding; the AI Doctor cannot palpate the abdomen and must not rely on a normal exam to exclude a proximal lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Digital rectal examination — normal tone, no palpable mass, trace bright-red blood on the glove (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal DRE excludes only a low, palpable rectal mass; the AI cannot perform a DRE and it does not assess the colon above the rectum.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full blood count, iron studies and ferritin

investigation_details.expected_result: May show iron-deficiency anaemia from chronic GI blood loss
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Undetected anaemia and missed quantification of blood loss; loss of an additional indication driving urgent colonoscopy.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bloods confirm or exclude anaemia and its severity; not obtainable via telehealth chat.

investigation_details.expected_result: Direct visualisation of the bleeding source; identification and removal of colorectal polyp(s) with histology to exclude malignancy
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The definitive test is not done — the source is never identified and an early colorectal cancer could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only endoscopy with histology can confirm the source and exclude cancer; impossible via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Recurrent rectal bleeding + change in bowel habit + possible iron-deficiency anaemia together mandate colonoscopy to exclude colorectal cancer, regardless of a reassuring remote picture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive investigation to identify and remove the bleeding source and exclude colorectal malignancy.
Quantifies chronic blood loss and confirms/excludes iron-deficiency anaemia; supports the urgency of colonoscopy.
